message:
perf(storage): rewrite ready-issues blocker filter as NOT EXISTS instead of NOT IN

`build_ready_issues_query` was filtering out blocked issues with:

  AND issues.id NOT IN (SELECT issue_id FROM blocked_issues_cache)

`NOT IN` against a subquery is fragile in two ways: (a) if any
`issue_id` in the subquery is NULL, the entire `NOT IN` expression
evaluates to UNKNOWN, which silently filters out every row even when
the issue isn't blocked; (b) the optimizer often materializes the
subquery into a temporary table rather than using an index lookup,
which scales linearly with the cache size.

Rewrite as a correlated `NOT EXISTS`:

  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM blocked_issues_cache
      WHERE blocked_issues_cache.issue_id = issues.id
  )

`NOT EXISTS` short-circuits on the first match per outer row,
behaves correctly under NULL (NULL never participates in the
equality match, so the outer row stays in the result), and lets the
optimizer use the existing `blocked_issues_cache(issue_id)` lookup
key directly. Same call sites, same semantics for non-NULL data,
robust under NULL, faster on large caches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
